title: Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
text:
This framework presents a generalized approach for assessing the performance of wastewater treatment systems using engineering principles, applied statistics, and reproducible analytical workflows.
The material is intended for educational and professional development purposes and does not represent any specific facility, organization, supplier, technology proponent, or regulatory assessment.

title: Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
text:
This framework presents a generalized approach for assessing the performance of wastewater treatment systems using engineering principles, applied statistics, and reproducible analytical workflows.
The material is intended for educational and professional development purposes and does not represent any specific facility, organization, supplier, technology proponent, or regulatory assessment.

title: Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
text:
Assessment Framework
The assessment process consists of the following stages:

Define assessment objectives.
Understand the treatment system.
Evaluate monitoring data quality.
Select performance indicators.
Apply descriptive statistical methods.
Assess trends and variability.
Evaluate treatment reliability.
Communicate uncertainty.
Develop engineering conclusions.
Document methods and assumptions.

title: Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
text:
Guiding Principle
Engineering assessments should be transparent, reproducible, evidence-based, and clearly communicate both conclusions and uncertainty.

title: Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
text:
Confidentiality
This framework presents generalized engineering methods only. Any examples used throughout this website are illustrative and do not represent actual facilities, organizations, clients, suppliers, manufacturers, technology proponents, or regulatory assessments.

title: Projects
text:
These projects present generalized engineering methods, analytical frameworks, and reproducible workflows for education and professional development.
All content is technology-neutral and does not reference confidential assessments, specific facilities, suppliers, organizations, or regulatory reviews.

title: Projects
text:
Project Catalogue


Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
Generalized methods for evaluating wastewater treatment system performance using engineering principles, applied statistics, and reproducible reporting.


Applied Statistics for Engineering
Practical statistical methods for environmental and civil engineering, including uncertainty, reliability, visualization, and interpretation.


Reproducible Technical Reporting
Transparent reporting workflows using Quarto, Python, R, Git, and GitHub.


Environmental Data Analytics
Methods for exploring, visualizing, and interpreting environmental monitoring data.


Technology Assessment Framework
Evidence-based approaches for evaluating environmental technologies using transparent and technology-neutral criteria.


Engineering Decision Support Tools
Frameworks and tools that support engineering decisions using data, statistics, uncertainty, and clear communication.

text: This project presents practical statistical methods that support evidence-based engineering decisions using environmental and engineering data.

title: Applied Statistics for Engineering
text:
Guiding Principle
Statistical analysis should improve engineering understanding rather than simply produce numerical results.

title: Interests
text: My current interests include sharing my statistical inquiries and technical reports I’ve worked that may help others solve similar problems related to applied statistics.

title: About
text:
I am an environmental engineer with over three decades of experience in wastewater engineering, environmental monitoring, applied statistics, and technical assessment.
My interests include integrating engineering judgment with statistical analysis to support transparent, evidence-based decision making. I am particularly interested in developing reproducible analytical workflows that improve technical communication and facilitate knowledge sharing across the engineering profession.




Ph.D.
Professional Engineer (P.Eng.)
Adjunct Professor, University of Guelph
Environmental Engineering
Applied Statistics
Technical Writing
Reproducible Research







Wastewater treatment, monitoring data, treatment system performance, and engineering interpretation.



Descriptive statistics, uncertainty, reliability, visualization, and practical statistical communication.



Quarto, R, Python, Git, GitHub, and transparent technical workflows.



Engineering education, professional development, plain-language technical writing, and student support.





Current areas of interest include:

Evidence-based environmental engineering
Treatment performance assessment
Applied statistics for engineering
Reproducible reporting using Quarto
Python and R for environmental data analysis
Engineering education and mentoring
Technical communication in plain language




Engineering decisions should be based on clear objectives, reliable data, appropriate analysis, transparent assumptions, and plain-language communication.
Good technical work should not only produce answers; it should also explain how those answers were developed, what uncertainty remains, and how the results should be interpreted.




Teach methods, not projects.
Use evidence before opinion.
Communicate uncertainty clearly.
Prefer reproducible workflows.
Keep public examples generalized, synthetic, or based on public information.
Avoid disclosing confidential, proprietary, or project-specific information.




This website is intended for educational and professional development purposes. It presents generalized engineering methods, statistical techniques, and reproducible workflows. Any examples are illustrative and do not represent specific facilities, organizations, clients, suppliers, manufacturers, technology proponents, or regulatory assessments.



For now, please use the GitHub link in the navigation bar.


The objective of this website is to advance environmental engineering through the sharing of public knowledge, evidence-based methods, and reproducible analytical workflows.

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text:
Welcome to Evidence-Based Engineering, a public knowledge base dedicated to advancing environmental engineering through evidence-based methods, applied statistics, and reproducible technical reporting.
This website shares generalized engineering frameworks, educational resources, and analytical workflows for engineers, students, researchers, and technical professionals.






NoteEducational and Professional Resource



This website is intended for educational and professional development purposes. It presents generalized engineering methods, statistical techniques, and reproducible workflows. Any examples are illustrative and do not represent specific facilities, organizations, clients, suppliers, manufacturers, technology proponents, or regulatory assessments.
